name: list_notes
description: List every markdown note path in the user's brain folder, sorted alphabetically. Use to discover what exists before writing, to avoid creating duplicates, and to scope a recall request.

name: search_notes
description: Find notes whose path or contents contain the query (case-insensitive substring match). Returns up to 50 hits with a one-line snippet from each. Use to recall prior context before answering, and to find existing topical pages before creating new ones.

query.description: Substring to search for. Try the user's exact words first, then variants if the first attempt returns nothing useful.

limit.description: Maximum number of hits to return. Defaults to 50.

name: read_note
description: Read the full markdown content of one note. Returns ok=false with error='not_found' if the file does not exist. ALWAYS call this before write_note when updating an existing note, otherwise write_note will overwrite history.

path.description: Relative path under the brain folder, e.g. 'daily/2026-05-09.md' or 'pages/Project Aurora.md'.

name: write_note
description: Write or overwrite a markdown note at the given path. REPLACES the entire file. Creates parent directories as needed. Use [[Page Name]] syntax to cross-link related notes.

path.description: Relative path under the brain folder. Must end in .md (will be appended if missing). Examples: 'daily/2026-05-09.md', 'pages/Sarah Johnson.md'.

content.description: Full markdown content of the note. This REPLACES any existing content. When updating, read_note first and pass the merged result here.
